originalTitle: 'I jumped around the house': Sabastian Sawe's parents celebrate marathon record
title: Sabastian Sawe Runs Super Fast! His Parents Jump for Joy!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Guess what? We have some very exciting news from our amazing continent, Africa! Get ready to hear about a super fast runner named Sabastian Sawe. He did something so incredible that his mom and dad were literally jumping up and down with happiness! Can you imagine being so happy you just can't stop jumping?

Who is Sabastian Sawe?

Sabastian Sawe is a very strong runner from Kenya. Kenya is a beautiful country in Africa, known for having some of the fastest runners in the whole wide world! Sabastian has been training very, very hard for many years. He loves to run, and he is very good at it. Running is like his superpower!

What Did Sabastian Do?

Sabastian ran a special race called a marathon. A marathon is not just any race; it is a SUPER long race! It's like running from your home to another town far, far away. Imagine running for hours and hours without stopping! That's what Sabastian did. Did you know? A marathon race is about 42 kilometers long! That's like running around your school field more than 100 times! It takes a lot of energy and strong legs to finish even one marathon. Sabastian didn't just run a marathon; he ran it super duper fast! He finished the race in less than two hours. That's what we call a 'sub-two-hour' marathon. It's a very, very special achievement because only a few people in the world have ever run that fast.

What Does 'Less Than Two Hours' Mean?

Think about this: Two hours is like watching two of your favorite cartoon movies back-to-back! Or it's like two full playtimes at school! Sabastian ran that super long race in the time it takes to watch those two movies. Isn't that amazing? He ran faster than almost anyone ever before!

Meet His Proud Parents: Emily and Simion Sawe

Now, let's talk about Sabastian's mom, Emily, and his dad, Simion. They are his biggest fans! Every time Sabastian runs, they cheer for him with all their hearts. They know how much hard work Sabastian puts into his running. They wake up early with him, encourage him, and make sure he eats healthy food to be strong. When Sabastian was racing that day, his parents were watching. Maybe they were watching on a big television screen, or listening on the radio. Their hearts must have been beating so fast, just like Sabastian's legs!

Jumping Around the House!

When Sabastian crossed the finish line and everyone knew he had run so incredibly fast, something wonderful happened at his parents' house! His mom, Emily, and his dad, Simion, couldn't hold back their happiness. They were so, so proud of their son. Emily said, "I jumped around the house!" Can you imagine? A grown-up jumping around the house like a happy little kid? That's how happy they were! Simion, Sabastian's dad, was just as excited. They clapped their hands, they cheered, and maybe they even did a happy dance! They felt a huge wave of pride. Pride means you feel very, very happy and good about someone because they did something amazing. They were proud of his speed, his strength, and all the hard work he put in to become such a champion.

Why is This a Big Deal for Africa?

Sabastian's amazing run makes everyone in Africa super proud! He shows us that with hard work, strong will, and big dreams, anything is possible. He is an inspiration to all the little boys and girls who love to run, or who have any big dream. He shows us that we, from Africa, can be the best in the world! Did you know? Many amazing long-distance runners come from East Africa, especially Kenya and Ethiopia. They often train in high places where the air is thin, which helps their bodies become even stronger!

How Does Sabastian Train?

To run so fast, Sabastian has to train every single day. He probably wakes up very early, even before the sun rises, to go for a run. He practices running uphill and downhill. He eats healthy food like fruits, vegetables, and good grains to give him energy. And he makes sure to get lots of sleep so his body can rest and get stronger. It's like when you practice drawing or playing a game; the more you practice, the better you become! Sabastian practiced a lot, and look what happened – he became a champion!

Dreams Can Come True!

Sabastian's story reminds us that if you have a dream, you should work hard for it. It might be a dream to run fast, or to draw beautiful pictures, or to learn to read many books. No matter what your dream is, if you work hard like Sabastian, you can make it come true. And when you do, your parents will be jumping around the house just like Emily and Simion Sawe! So, let's cheer for Sabastian Sawe, our African running hero! He makes us all proud and shows us the power of dreams and hard work. What a fantastic story!
summary: African runner Sabastian Sawe made his parents, Emily and Simion Sawe, incredibly proud by finishing a super long marathon race in less than two hours, causing them to jump with joy around their house.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows that with lots of hard work and big dreams, anyone can achieve amazing things, making their families and all of Africa very proud, just like Sabastian Sawe did.
